auth_notes: Requires the Domino Data Service (DDS) or the newer HCL Domino REST API (Project KEEP) to be enabled on the server. Authentication typically uses standard credentials or a session cookie obtained via a login endpoint.
rate_limits: Controlled at the server level via Domino's Internet Site documents or Server documents; no fixed global limit.
gotchas: The Domino Data Service (DDS) must be manually enabled for each specific database (.nsf) and view.
Data is often returned in a flat JSON structure that requires significant mapping to handle multi-value fields.
On-premise deployments often require a VPN or n8n tunnel/agent to bypass firewalls.
client_must_provide: Firewall whitelisting for n8n IP addresses
research_notes: Integration requires the HTTP Request node. If using the legacy Domino Data Service, the URL pattern is typically /database.nsf/api/data/documents. If using the modern HCL Domino REST API (KEEP), it provides a more standard Swagger/OpenAPI experience but requires separate installation on the Domino server.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration requires the HTTP Request node. If using the legacy Domino Data Service, the URL pattern is typically /database.nsf/api/data/documents. If using the modern HCL Domino REST API (KEEP), it provides a more standard Swagger/OpenAPI experience but requires separate installation on the Domino server.
